test.command: echo 'NOTE: ypricemagic test suite does not reliably run on macOS. Validation happens through ypricemagic-server Docker stack.'
test.note: Tests cannot run on macOS per services.yaml; validation is through the server Docker stack. This is a known pre-existing constraint, not a new failure.

typecheck.note: mypy reports 1770 errors across 91 files — identical in scope and nature to the pre-existing errors documented in the stablecoin-pricing scrutiny synthesis. The errors span unrelated modules (cli.py, logging.py, _db/, etc.) and are caused by pre-existing architectural issues (brownie type stubs, dank_mids exports, typed_envs). None trace to files introduced or modified in the exotic-tokens milestone (curve_gauge.py, erc4626.py, exotic_tokens.py, buckets.py, magic.py, aave.py, one_to_one.py). Treated as pre-existing per prior synthesis precedent.

lint.output: All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨ 163 files would be left unchanged.

blockingIssues.description: test_gauge_bucket_detection uses SDAI_USDM_GAUGE (0xcF5136C67fA8A375BaBbDf13c0307EF994b5681D) as test address, but this same address is hardcoded in y/prices/one_to_one.py. check_bucket() evaluates string_matchers (including 'one to one') BEFORE calls_only (including 'curve gauge'), so the test will assert 'curve gauge' but receive 'one to one'. The test itself is self-contradictory — test_hardcoded_gauge_still_works acknowledges this ordering. Fix: replace SDAI_USDM_GAUGE in test_gauge_bucket_detection with a gauge address NOT in one_to_one.py.

PoolTogether V4 ticket detection is broken: is_pool_together_v4_ticket() passes getTwab(address,uint32) to has_methods(), but has_methods only works for no-input view methods (contracts.py:839). It always returns False for input-bearing methods. Fix: Remove getTwab from has_methods call. Use only controller()(address) for detection, then verify name matches 'PoolTogether * Ticket' pattern separately.

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Token Detection Patterns' section documenting: (1) has_methods only works for no-input view methods — never use with input-bearing methods like getTwab(address,uint32); (2) Bucket evaluation order (string_matchers before calls_only) — test addresses for calls_only buckets must not overlap with one_to_one.py MAPPING; (3) ZERO_ADDRESS guard pattern for raw_call address returns.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add rule: @stuck_coro_debugger must be applied to is_* detection functions as well as get_price_* functions, following the yearn.py pattern (@a_sync.a_sync → @stuck_coro_debugger → @optional_async_diskcache). Detection functions make multiple RPC calls and can stall just as pricing functions can.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: y/prices/exotic_tokens.py is_pickle_pslp, is_pool_together_v4_ticket, is_xpremia, is_xtarot, is_tarot_supply_vault — all missing @stuck_coro_debugger. y/prices/yearn.py:77 has the correct pattern. Two separate reviewers flagged this across erc4626-vault-improvement and exotic-token-parity-and-detection.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: When writing unit tests for detection functions that use calls_only bucket detection, verify that the chosen test address is NOT also present in string_matchers (especially one_to_one.py MAPPING). Addresses in one_to_one.py will always resolve to 'one to one' bucket before calls_only is evaluated. Test addresses for calls_only buckets must be algorithmically-detectable-only (not hardcoded anywhere).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: curve-gauge-support blocking issue: SDAI_USDM_GAUGE in test_gauge_bucket_detection is also in one_to_one.py, so the test will always fail in CI.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about complex async mock patching: tests that use bare `except Exception: pass` around core behavioral assertions provide false confidence. When ERC20 property patching fails, the test silently passes without verifying the behavior. Prefer patching at a higher level (e.g., patch _call_preview_redeem directly) or use pytest.raises / mark xfail.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tests/test_erc4626.py:143, 228, 276 — three tests with bare except-pass wrapping key assertions. Worker's own skillFeedback identified this as a pain point.

rejectedObservations.observation: Stale TODO comment in one_to_one.py about 'algorithmically get gauges'
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking minor cleanup. Not a correctness issue. Can be addressed in a follow-up commit without blocking validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: get_lp_token() in curve_gauge.py: no None-guard before passing to magic.get_price()
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking. In practice, get_price() is only called after is_curve_gauge() which confirms lp_token is not None. Defensive guard is a nice-to-have, not blocking.

rejectedObservations.observation: Tarot vault pricing uses hardcoded scale = 10**18 instead of fetching actual decimals
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking. All known Tarot contracts use 18 decimals. Adding dynamic decimal fetch would require an additional RPC call. Risk is extremely low in practice.
